chapter_title.ar: باب الْأَوَانِي يَكُونُ فِيهَا الْخَمْرُ ثُمَّ يُجْعَلُ فِيهَا الْخَلُّ أَوْ يُشْرَبُ بِهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 33 – The utensils in which happens to be the wine, then the vinegar is made to be in it, or is drunk by it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/295)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/295)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الدَّنُّ تَكُونُ فِيهِ الْخَمْرُ ثُمَّ يُجَفَّفُ يُجْعَلُ فِيهِ الْخَلُّ قَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, and Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad altogether, from Al Hajjal, from Sa’alba, from Hafs Al Awr who said,
‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The vat in which the wine happens to be, then it dries up, so the vinegar is made to be in it’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: Yes’.
en.sarwar: 2. Abu Ali al-Ash‘ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar and Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad all from al-Hajj al from Tha’labah from Hafs ibn al-A‘war who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a large jug in which there is wine, then it dries up if vinegar can be kept in it. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, one can do so.’”
